headline: Sandisk and Micron stocks sink as rotation trade builds
detail: Chips are explicitly being sold today as part of a broader rotation trade. This directly pressures SMH and SOXL in your book - SOXL at 3x leverage means any further leg down hits you three times as hard. The note that supply shortages should limit losses is a small comfort but not a reason to add.
rec: Watch SOXL's $200 stop closely today given direct sector pressure. No new chip buys - MU shows a scanner setup (entry 1046-1045, stop 1038) but buying into an active rotation sell-off on margin is not the day for it.

headline: U.S. won't renew USMCA, opening door for negotiations
detail: Trade uncertainty with Canada and Mexico adds macro noise that historically rattles risk assets and tech supply chains. Chip and hardware names are exposed to cross-border supply dynamics, adding a background headwind to your semiconductor-heavy book.
rec: No action triggered directly, but this is another reason not to add new leveraged chip exposure today - monitor whether this escalates into something that moves SMH or QQQ meaningfully.

headline: June employment report coming Friday
detail: A stronger-than-expected jobs number would push back rate-cut expectations, which is negative for high-multiple tech and QQQ. Your whole book is ~90% tech/chip so a hot print Friday could hit everything at once, and margin means drawdowns compound fast.
rec: No trade today, but consider whether your margin cushion is comfortable heading into Friday's print. If QQQ breaks toward $670 stop on a hot jobs number, that's a forced decision you want to make now, not in the moment.

headline: Palantir's Karp bashes OpenAI and Anthropic token model
detail: Karp publicly attacking the dominant AI model providers creates noise around AI infrastructure narratives that underpin many of your chip names' valuations. Not a direct mover today but adds sentiment uncertainty to the AI-premium built into names like CRWD and the broader QQQ complex.
rec: No action warranted - this is commentary, not a fundamental change. CRWD is your only AI-adjacent name up meaningfully (+6.65%) and is holding fine; no reason to trim or add based on this headline alone.
